replacement_target: later explicit XStack/AIDE scope checkpoint or replacement artifact only

current_program_context.current_product_priority: repo-local playable baseline and repo stabilization
current_program_context.series_role: narrowing, classification, and contract extraction preparation that must not compete with playable-baseline work

current_xstack_definition.summary: XStack is the live repo-native deterministic tooling and control layer centered on tools/xstack/, plus its immediate support docs/data, used today for gate orchestration, validation families, session artifact materialization/control, pack and registry tooling, CI guardrails, and related operator surfaces.
better_understood_as_future_aide_materia: any standalone repo-independent extraction of XStack contracts into a new platform identity
any AIDE runtime, daemon, compiler, adapter ecosystem, or service layer
any portability claim that depends on XStack already being cleanly separable from Dominium today
evidence_markers: tools/xstack/run.py is a live XStack entrypoint
tools/xstack/controlx/orchestrator.py coordinates pack, bundle, compile, audit, test, session, and UI checks
tools/xstack/sessionx/** is a live implementation surface for session lifecycle and process/session control
validation/validation_engine.py imports and maps XStack compatx surfaces directly
appshell/**, server/server_boot.py, and client/local_server/local_server_controller.py import tools.xstack helpers
repo search found live tools.xstack references under engine/, game/, client/, and server/
repo search found no current repo-local AIDE implementation root
current_xstack_definition.conservative_interpretation: XStack is live and important in the current repo, but it is not yet proven to be a clean standalone platform.

portable_core.meaning: Narrow deterministic substrate that already exposes generic planner, runner, hashing, or schema-contract behavior with limited direct product knowledge.
inclusion_criteria: structurally generic
reused across multiple XStack families
describable without Dominium session or startup assumptions

runtime_concern.meaning: XStack-branded code that directly creates, boots, stages, controls, or mutates sessions or authoritative session-adjacent state.
inclusion_criteria: works with SessionSpec or universe artifacts
handles SRZ, stage parity, observation/render adaptation, or authoritative process/session control
directly affects session lifecycle rather than only validating or auditing it

ops_concern.meaning: Operator, CI, gate, audit, validation, and repo-execution surfaces.
inclusion_criteria: runs the repo or gates changes
emits evidence or validation output
coordinates verification rather than product gameplay

dominium_specific_concern.meaning: Surfaces tied to Dominium product shells, bundle/lock/profile assumptions, loopback authority, or repo-local control-plane details.
inclusion_criteria: bakes in Dominium paths or bundle IDs
depends on registry outputs or release/trust wiring specific to this repo
assumes product-shell behavior not proven portable

legacy_transitional.meaning: Older sibling roots, wrappers, or docs that remain useful evidence but are not the current XStack semantic center.
inclusion_criteria: predates current tools/xstack/ canon
is treated as wrapper or transitional by audit evidence
overclaims portability or removability relative to live code

deferred_to_later_aide_extraction.meaning: Plausible future extraction candidates that may be mapped now but must not be platformized now.
inclusion_criteria: shows reusable structure
would require additional uncoupling or convergence work before extraction
extraction now would compete with playable-baseline assembly

explicitly_outside_current_scope.meaning: Work this series must not perform now.
inclusion_criteria: product or runtime expansion not required for scope freeze
non-loopback multiplayer or infrastructure expansion
broad renaming, restructuring, or not-yet-present AIDE implementation

portable_core.rationale: plan.py, scheduler.py, runners.py, and artifact_contract.py implement a generic deterministic planner, runner, cache, and artifact-classification substrate with limited direct product knowledge.
Pure canonical JSON serialization and hashing helper reused broadly without product-shell semantics.
Strict schema validator for canonical contracts; reusable contract substrate rather than gameplay or product runtime.
Generic schema discovery and version-registry loading helper; repo-relative today, but structurally a contract-layer primitive.

runtime_concern.rationale: creator.py, runner.py, scheduler.py, process_runtime.py, pipeline_contract.py, ui_host.py, and related modules implement the XStack-branded session lifecycle and authoritative process/session machinery.
Creates session_spec.json, universe_identity.json, universe_state.json, and universe_contract_bundle.json; this is session materialization, not just generic tooling.
Boots SessionSpec artifacts and is audited as a real but fragile runtime/session path.
Stage, abort, resume, compact, and CLI/TUI/GUI parity wrappers are session-control surfaces.
Scripted SRZ and session/server status handling are runtime/session operations.
Validates ui.registry descriptors against sessionx.ui_host expectations and session gating, so it belongs with session/runtime support rather than pure ops.

ops_concern.rationale: Stable FAST/STRICT/FULL XStack entrypoint for deterministic profile runs.
orchestrator.py coordinates pack, bundle, compile, audit, test, session, and UI checks as repo operations.
Deterministic test harness and large suite selection are validation and evidence operations.
Audit, static checks, trust checks, and profile or budget checks are operator-facing gate families.
These surfaces define and drive CI and guardrail policy rather than gameplay or product runtime.
Imports tools.xstack.core.* and functionally participates in the live XStack control layer even though it does not live under tools/xstack/.
Unified validation surface that consumes XStack compatx contracts and emits audit or validation evidence.
Profile-driven CompatX check surface is an operational contract-check entrypoint rather than a low-level primitive.

dominium_specific_concern.rationale: Uses default bundle IDs such as bundle.base.lab and emits a large Dominium registry set; current structure is tied to Dominium bundle and registry vocabulary.
Assume Dominium pack roots, manifest names, and contribution vocabulary.
Dist layout and packaging helpers are tied to Dominium packaging and release structure.
Product bootstrap, supervisor, IPC, compatibility, and virtual-path logic import XStack helpers but remain product-shell integration.
Directly imports tools.xstack.sessionx.* and pack or session helpers; server boot remains Dominium-owned even when it uses XStack helpers.
Loopback local-singleplayer controller is baseline gameplay glue, not current AIDE extraction material.
Live baseline content and config pair plus session template called out by the audit as the near-term playable slice.

legacy_transitional.rationale: Older autonomous control-plane wrapper outside tools/xstack/; still useful evidence, but not the current branded XStack root.
Older compatibility CLI and core; validation/validation_engine.py maps it as a legacy or adapter surface rather than the current XStack canon.
Explicitly represented by the unified validation engine as legacy coverage adapters or deprecated aggregate validator surfaces.
Audit marks this as a transitional wrapper rather than the dependable canonical playtest bootstrap.
Useful reference, but still describes an older gate-centric model and does not cleanly reflect current live coupling.
Derived portability and removability guidance; several files say they are only partially aligned, and live imports show stronger coupling than the docs claim.

deferred_to_later_aide_extraction.rationale: Strongest reusable contract substrate, but extracting it now would still require uncoupling discipline and would compete with baseline assembly.
Coherent tool families that may later support AIDE-style extraction, but current scope is documentation, mapping, and contract freezing only.
Useful support surfaces for later extracted policy or catalog packaging, but docs and data do not justify a standalone platform by themselves.
Plausible later convergence target after scope is frozen, but still a sibling and transitional surface now.

explicitly_outside_current_scope.rationale: No such repo roots exist; a full AIDE implementation is not present and is not authorized by this series.
These roots may be read as evidence, but XStack/AIDE work may not broaden into general product or runtime implementation work here.
Audit evidence says non-loopback multiplayer transport is not part of the near-term playable baseline.
Audit evidence says compiled launcher and setup shells should stay wrappers for now.
Broad convergence or rename work here would compete with playable-baseline assembly and exceeds scope-freeze work.

in_scope_now: freeze the current repo-grounded meaning of XStack
inventory and classify branded XStack surfaces and adjacent sibling roots that materially interact with them
identify conservative portable-contract candidates without claiming full portability
define the boundary between current XStack, Dominium-owned product or runtime surfaces, and later AIDE candidates
extract documentation-level or machine-readable contract maps for later prompts
define a repo-operating contract for later agent or Codex work over XStack/AIDE surfaces
map what should remain in Dominium versus what may later be reviewed for extraction
produce scope, inventory, classification, and extraction-mapping artifacts that do not alter runtime or product behavior
out_of_scope_now: implement a compiler, adapter runtime, or bakeoff framework
perform broad repo renaming for its own sake
move tools/xstack/** or adjacent roots just to make the tree look cleaner
implement multiplayer transport beyond the current loopback baseline
replace launcher, setup, or product shells instead of documenting the stronger live repo-local paths
treat portability docs as proof that the live repo is already uncoupled
reopen blocked or future-only post-Zeta frontier work

later_aide_candidates.why_later_not_now: Strongest generic execution substrate, but extraction should wait until after baseline stabilization and explicit uncoupling review.
Contract helpers are reusable, but the live repo still couples them to Dominium validation and execution surfaces.
These are coherent tool families, but current scope is to classify and map them rather than platformize them.
Support docs and catalogs can mirror later extraction decisions, but they do not by themselves justify creating a new platform surface.
Potential convergence target after semantics are frozen; still transitional and not the current semantic center.

definitely_dominium_owned.why: These are tied to current session lifecycle, universe artifacts, SRZ, and authoritative runtime behavior.
Product-shell bootstrap, supervision, IPC, compatibility, and virtual-path behavior remain Dominium-specific.
These provide baseline loopback authority and local product glue rather than a portable externalized platform boundary.
These artifacts define the current repo-local playable baseline slice.
These are tied to Dominium bundle, pack, and packaging vocabulary and should remain repo-owned for now.

ambiguous_or_mixed.why: These are operationally XStack-adjacent and consume XStack contracts, but they are also broader repo validation infrastructure rather than purely XStack-owned.
This is session/runtime-adjacent today, but parts of its descriptor-validation behavior may later prove portable.
These remain useful for intent and historical framing, but they overstate current removability or extraction readiness relative to live code.

rules: doctrinal similarity does not equal extraction readiness
implementation existence does not equal portability
current utility inside Dominium does not equal future AIDE ownership
support docs or mirrors do not override live repo implementation evidence
later extraction candidates may be mapped now without changing current ownership

prohibited_scope_expansion_shapes: renaming roots before semantics are frozen
using future AIDE ambitions to justify present repo churn
promoting derived portability prose above live implementation evidence
collapsing doctrine, implementation, and extraction into one claim

downstream_prompts_enabled.purpose: inventory and classification refinement using the frozen scope buckets and boundaries
portable-contract extraction mapping for conservative core and ops surfaces
Codex or repo-operating contract formalization for XStack/AIDE work that respects Dominium ownership boundaries
future post-baseline review of whether any deferred candidates are mature enough for actual extraction
